title: drosera occidentalis var. microscapa
intro: Drosera occidentalis var. microscapa, also known as Western Sundew, is a carnivorous plant species that obtains essential nutrients by capturing and digesting insects. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Western Sundew include relief from congestion, coughs, and sore throats, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. Western Sundew can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
